<div style="text-align:center; font-size:1.6em">

[{{< iconify bi mortarboard >}}](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0kUG_e4AAAAJ&hl=en) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify bi journal-bookmark >}}](https://osf.io/e834b/) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify bi person-badge >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3649-542) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify fa researchgate >}}](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mohammed-Mudarris) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify bi linkedin >}}](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mamudarris/) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify bi share >}}](https://bsky.app/profile/mamudarris.bsky.social) &nbsp;  
[{{< iconify bi envelope >}}](mailto:mamudarris@gmail.com)

</div>
---
title: "Mohammed Mudarris"
---
![](Self_portrait.JPG){width=220px}

PhD Candidate  
Health, Medical, Neuropsychology |  
Institute of Psychology |  
Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences |  
Leiden University

My research focuses on neuroplasticity of motor learning with multisensory stimulation in aging and neurological disease. Using multimodal MRI methods, I examine structural, functional, and network-level brain changes associated with motor learning and rehabilitation.  

## Research Interests

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Multimodal Neuroimaging  
- Motor learning & cognition  
- Aging and stroke  
- Multisensory stimulation  

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Music-based rehabilitation  
- Cognitive-motor interference  
- Hierarchical modelling  

:::

::::

---
